other_observers: I first found the bird on 2/4/2011 with Elena Klaver, Hannah Floyd, and Andrew Floyd. I suspect the 2/4/2011 bird was the same as the 2/6/2011 bird. Regarding the 1/8/2011 bird, it was first spotted and identified by Bryan Guarente, then seen by many. (My glimpse of that bird was inadequate for submission to the Colorado Bird Records Committee.); Yes.
specific_location: Valmont Reservoir proper. Just beyond the chain link fence on the northwest side of the "peninsula" that juts in from Red Deer Drive.
description: Note: I saw this bird on 2 dates. This description is based on my sighting on the 2nd day of viewing, because I saw the bird better that day.  The bird was a straightforward basic adult Mew Gull. Seen in the general vicinity of Ring-billed Gulls, it was smaller, daintier, and darker-mantled. It had a light to moderate (for a basic Mew Gull) brown streaking on the nape. Its dainty bill looked to be entirely yellow. Its staring black eye stood out on its small, rounded head. Its long wing tips were mainly black, with large white spots.Behaviors: Standing on ice. It was generally in front of a large mass of Ring-billed Gulls, although a few Ring-billeds were standing to its left and right, i.e., the same distance from me.  At one point, it flew off, although I did not see this happen.Call: none heard.Plumage: Basic adult
